briefing.headline: Rig up a 10" Worm — aggressive spawn approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The 10" Worm is a strong choice for spawn with clear water and pre frontal conditions.

For Yamamoto 10-inch grub: use Gamakatsu EWG Superline 5/0 hook with screw-in 1/4oz weight (F rig) and delayed hookset. Straight shank hooks cause missed fish

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

primaryApproach.retrieve: For Yamamoto 10-inch grub: use Gamakatsu EWG Superline 5/0 hook with screw-in 1/4oz weight (F rig) and delayed hookset. Straight shank hooks cause missed fish
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on flat, grass, hump. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Palaniuk: 'Start with a Texas-rigged 10-inch worm in brushpiles because the heavier line and bigger hook up the odds of landing fish in cover, then switch to Neko rig for more bites'
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on flat, grass, hump. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: KVD: On clear water grass lakes, most anglers fish the inside line where fish spawn. But fish also spawn in the deeper scattered grass patches (7ft+) in the middle of big flats. Nobody else fishes it — that's a competitive advantage. A lot of larg...

Johnston: You have to be able to judge the size of bedding fish without catching them to make sure you're going to get the right ones. Spend time gathering waypoints on spawning areas before tournament day.
Palaniuk: 'I like to fish offshore and target shell bars or brushpiles with a 10-inch worm in summer.'

Best for pitching to isolated cover - docks, logs, laydowns, boulders, stumps. A little tough to sink into thick cover. Effective on both smallmouth and largemouth, spawning, cruising, and fry guarding fish

briefing.depthStrategy: Pre-frontal spawning bass are active on beds in 2-5ft. Falling pressure often triggers new waves of fish to move onto beds. Target hard-bottom spawning flats, secondary pockets, and protected coves.
adjustIf: If the front stalls and conditions stabilize, dial back aggression and focus on higher-percentage structure
If water clarity decreases from wind or rain, upsize and switch to brighter colors
If bites dry up, try shaking in place for 10-15 seconds before moving — patience pays with plastics
